name: sub_agent
description: Spawn a child session (same agent as you) to handle a delegated task. The child runs in its OWN isolated session — its intermediate tool calls, thinking, and partial messages are NOT forwarded to your context. You only ever see the child's FINAL reply. Sync mode (run_in_background=false, default): this call blocks your turn until the child replies or the timeout fires, and the reply is returned directly as the tool result. Background mode (run_in_background=true): this call returns immediately with a task id; continue with your own work — when the child finishes, its final reply is automatically injected into your context as a new message. The runtime handles delivery end-to-end; no manual inspection of the child's files is needed. Use 'explorer' mode for investigation tasks (read-only outside the child's playground/; safe defaults), and 'executor' mode when the child needs full write access (no sandbox).

name.description: Short display name for the child session (≤ 40 chars). Shown as the session label in the sidebar and in the sub_agent panel card so sibling children are distinguishable. Pick something task-descriptive, e.g. 'audit auth flow' or 'port kimi tests'.

task.description: The task for the child agent. This becomes the child's first user_input — write it as you would write a clear, complete prompt to a fresh agent. Cite paths and constraints; do not assume the child has any context from your conversation.

mode.description: explorer = sandboxed (writes only inside the child's playground/; safe for research and untrusted exploration). executor = no sandbox; use only when the child legitimately needs to modify shared files.

timeout_seconds.description: Sync-mode wait timeout (default 600s). On timeout the sync call returns a timeout notice; the child keeps running and its final reply still arrives later via the background-completion path.
